stories.title: Truist Championship R1 — McCarty Leads at 8-Under, McIlroy 7 Back After Flat Round

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Matt McCarty: 8-under 63. Leading the Truist Championship.

Made a 52-footer at 13. A 59-footer at 16. Another 52-footer at 17.

220 feet of putts made. Quail Hollow had no answers. #TruistChampionship

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Rory McIlroy made 17 consecutive pars.

Then birdied his last hole.

Final score: 70. Seven shots off the lead at his favorite course.

The record 5th Quail Hollow title is going to need a round for the ages in R2. #TruistChampionship

article.headline: McCarty's 220-Foot Putting Exhibition Leads Truist Championship — McIlroy Seven Back After Flat Round 1

image_concepts.label: Image 1A
image_concepts.usage: McCarty celebrating birdie or finishing round

image_concepts.label: Image 1B
image_concepts.usage: McIlroy on the course at Quail Hollow

research.facts:
Truist Championship Round 1
- **HIGH**: Matt McCarty shot 8-under 63, leading Truist Championship R1 at Quail Hollow
- **HIGH**: One shot ahead of Sungjae Im (who had one hole incomplete at rain delay)
- **HIGH**: McCarty made 220 feet of putts — most by a player on Tour since 2025 Scottish Open
- **HIGH**: McCarty made 52-foot birdie at 13th, 59-foot at 16th, 52-foot at 17th
- **HIGH**: McCarty gained more than 4 strokes on the greens
- **HIGH**: Five players at 5-under T3: Sepp Straka, Kristoffer Reitan, Nicolai Højgaard, Harry Hall, Nick Taylor
- **HIGH**: Cameron Young and Tommy Fleetwood at 4-under
- **HIGH**: McIlroy shot 1-under 70, seven shots back
- **HIGH**: McIlroy made 17 consecutive pars before his sole birdie at final hole (par-4 9th)
- **HIGH**: Round delayed by 2+ inches of rain Wednesday night/Thursday morning
- **MEDIUM**: McIlroy quoted that ball-striking was "not good enough" (PGA Tour coverage, multiple outlets)

stories.title: LIV Golf Virginia R1 — Herbert Shoots 64 to Lead, Rahm and DeChambeau 5 Back

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Lucas Herbert: 64. Two-shot lead at LIV Golf Virginia.

Made 52-foot birdie. Led Ripper GC to 4-shot team lead.

Rahm and DeChambeau are 5 back. They'll be hunting him down today. #LIVGolf

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Bryson DeChambeau came back from a wrist injury to shoot 3-under 69 in R1 at LIV Virginia.

Jon Rahm? Also 69.

Both 5 behind Herbert. If they find their form today, this is their tournament to take. #LIVGolf

article.headline: Herbert Shoots 64 to Lead LIV Golf Virginia — Rahm and DeChambeau Five Back After Opening Round

image_concepts.label: Image 2B
image_concepts.usage: Rahm on the tee or walking fairway

research.facts:
LIV Golf Virginia Round 1
- **HIGH**: Lucas Herbert shot 8-under 64 to lead
- **HIGH**: Two shots ahead of Marc Leishman, Sergio Garcia, Tyrrell Hatton, Sebastián Muñoz (all at 66)
- **HIGH**: Jon Rahm and Bryson DeChambeau both shot 3-under 69, five behind
- **HIGH**: DeChambeau returning from wrist injury (missed final round of previous LIV event in Mexico)
- **HIGH**: Ripper GC (Herbert's team) leads team leaderboard by 4 shots
- **HIGH**: Tournament at Trump National Golf Club DC (Sterling, VA)

stories.title: PGA Championship Field Complete — Scheffler Skips Truist to Prepare for Aronimink

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Scottie Scheffler skipped the Truist Championship this week.

His reason: focused preparation for the PGA Championship at Aronimink (May 14-17).

Three straight runner-up finishes. He wants a win — and he's treating this major like a mission. #PGAChampionship

article.headline: Scheffler Skips Truist to Prepare for Aronimink — PGA Championship Field Set for Donald Ross Classic

research.facts:
PGA Championship Field
- **HIGH**: Aronimink Golf Club, Newtown Square, PA. May 14-17
- **HIGH**: Scheffler deliberately skipped Truist Championship for Aronimink preparation
- **HIGH**: 154-player field, 29 major champions, 15 PGA Champions
- **HIGH**: Last PGA Championship at Aronimink: Gary Player won in 1962
- **HIGH**: LIV stars in field: DeChambeau, Rahm, Dustin Johnson
- **HIGH**: Two spots held for Myrtle Beach Classic and Truist Championship winners
- **HIGH**: Three par-3s over 210 yards at Aronimink
- **HIGH**: McIlroy playing at Aronimink

stories.title: Nelly Korda at 18 Career Wins — Now the Youngest American to Reach That Mark Since Nancy Lopez

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Nelly Korda now has 18 LPGA career wins.

Youngest American to reach 18 wins since Nancy Lopez was 23 (1980).

4 wins in 2026. Nothing worse than a runner-up through her first 6 starts.

She is simply on another level. #LPGA #NellyKorda

article.headline: Korda's 18th Career Win Places Her Among the All-Time Greats — Youngest American to Reach That Mark Since Nancy Lopez

image_concepts.label: Image 4A
image_concepts.usage: Korda celebrating win or holding trophy

research.facts:
Nelly Korda Career Records
- **HIGH**: Korda won Riviera Maya Open at Mayakoba for 18th career LPGA title
- **HIGH**: Back-to-back wins (Chevron Championship + Riviera Maya)
- **HIGH**: Won Riviera Maya by 4 shots at 17-under total
- **HIGH**: Youngest American with 18 LPGA wins since Nancy Lopez at age 23 in 1980
- **HIGH**: 4 wins in 2026 (including Chevron Championship = 3rd major)
- **HIGH**: First player since Annika Sorenstam (2001) to start season with nothing worse than runner-up through 6 events
- **HIGH**: Next start: Queen City Championship, Cincinnati, May 14-17

stories.title: McIlroy Must Rally — Quail Hollow Record Bid in Jeopardy After Quiet R1

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Rory McIlroy trails by 7 at Quail Hollow.

He's won here 4 times. He knows this course better than anyone.

But 17 straight pars in Round 1 won't cut it if he wants that record 5th title.

Round 2 needs to be special. #TruistChampionship

article.headline: McIlroy's Record 5th Quail Hollow Title Hangs by a Thread — Seven Back After Forgettable Round 1

research.facts:
McIlroy Quail Hollow Record
- **HIGH**: McIlroy seeking record 5th Truist Championship / Quail Hollow win
- **HIGH**: Sitting at 1-under 70 after R1, seven shots behind leader
- **HIGH**: Made 17 consecutive pars before birdie at final hole
- **MEDIUM**: "Not good enough" ball-striking quote (reported widely)
## Confidence Notes
- McIlroy's specific quote about ball-striking is MEDIUM — paraphrased in several sources but phrasing may vary
- LIV Golf team leaderboard lead of "4 shots" is HIGH based on two independent sources (LIV official, Washington Times)
